mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader equity valuations through financial sector stress and reduced construction activity, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and strong corporate output, which directly supports earnings growth and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 20-day returns in >50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp supply-side shock and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in less than 40% of instances where industrial production falls below the -0.5% threshold.
